description: Design a distributed API rate limiter for a multi-instance gateway. Each gateway instance must enforce per-user rate limits of 100 req/minute. The system runs 8 gateway pods, each handling requests independently. When a user sends a request, the gateway must check the current request count for that user and reject the request if it exceeds 100, otherwise increment the counter and allow the request.
context: The system uses Redis 7 as the shared rate limit store. Each gateway pod can reach Redis directly. The rate limit window is a rolling 60-second window. Counter keys expire after 60 seconds.

checks.text: Does the proposal use a single atomic Redis operation (INCR, or a Lua script, or MULTI/EXEC) to check-and-increment the counter — not a separate GET followed by a conditional SET or INCR?
Does the proposal correctly handle the distributed case where multiple gateway pods access the same Redis counter concurrently — ensuring the rate limit is enforced globally, not per-pod?
Does the proposal set an expiry (EXPIRE or EXPIREAT) on the counter key so that the rate limit window resets after 60 seconds?

should_prune.description: Proposal that reads counter with GET, checks if < 100, then increments with INCR or SET as two separate commands
should_prune.violates: SR/1 — TOCTOU race: two pods can both read count=99 and both allow the request, causing overage

should_prune.description: Proposal that maintains per-pod counters and aggregates them periodically
should_prune.violates: SR/2 — eventual consistency allows bursts exceeding the global limit between sync intervals

should_pass: Proposal using atomic Redis INCR: (1) INCR user:{user_id}:rate_limit — atomically increments and returns new value; (2) if new value == 1: EXPIRE user:{user_id}:rate_limit 60 — sets TTL on first increment; (3) if new value > 100: reject request (optionally DECR to roll back). This is atomic because INCR returns the value after increment in a single operation. No GET is needed.
